human:
POINTS:
- [auto-value-moshi/src/test/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtensionTest.java:115] Moshi’s `adapter()` method below trusts you to get your types right. But here you’re slightly cheating, coercing a `Set<String>` to a `Set<? extends String>`. In practice it’s the same, but it isn’t guaranteed in general.

Might be better to just add the `Types.subtypeOf()`.
v1:
SUMMARY:
PR adds wildcard type handling (`? extends T` / `? super T`) to the `makeType` method in `AutoValueMoshiExtension.java`, generating `Types.subtypeOf()` / `Types.supertypeOf()` calls instead of invalid inlined wildcard syntax.

POINTS:
- [auto-value-moshi/src/main/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtension.java:503-505] The multi-bound error check only guards `upperBounds.size() != 1`, but `WildcardTypeName` can have `lowerBounds.size() == 1` AND `upperBounds.size() > 1` (e.g., `? super T extends A & B`). The lower-bound branch would succeed and generate `Types.supertypeOf(T.class)` silently dropping the additional upper bounds. Consider adding a check: `if (wildcard.lowerBounds.size() == 1 && wildcard.upperBounds.size() > 1)` to reject this case.
- [auto-value-moshi/src/main/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtension.java:500-501] Code checks `lowerBounds.size() == 1` before `upperBounds.size() == 1`. Since a valid `WildcardTypeName` has either lower bounds (with `upperBounds` defaulted to `[Object.class]`) or only upper bounds, an unbounded `?` would match `upperBounds.size() == 1` and produce `Types.subtypeOf(Object.class)` — which is correct semantically, but the lower-bound-first ordering makes the logic slightly confusing. A comment clarifying that lower bounds are checked first because they are mutually exclusive with explicit upper bounds would help.
- [auto-value-moshi/src/main/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtension.java:506] The code generates `Types.subtypeOf($T.class)` and `Types.supertypeOf($T.class)` at runtime. These Moshi `Types` methods have no prior usage in this codebase (only `Types.newParameterizedType()` is used). Verify these factory methods exist in the Moshi version declared in `dependencies.gradle` (`1.4.0`). If they were added in a later Moshi version, this will produce compilation errors in generated code.
- [auto-value-moshi/src/test/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtensionTest.java:75-77] The existing `simple()` test was modified to use `Set<? extends String>` and `Map<String, Set<? super String>>` instead of `Set<String>` and `Map<String, Set<String>>`. This means the test no longer exercises non-wildcard type arguments within `newParameterizedType`. Consider keeping the original non-wildcard cases in the test or adding a separate test for them to avoid regression in the non-wildcard path.
- [auto-value-moshi/src/test/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtensionTest.java:126] No test covers the unbounded wildcard case (`Set<?>`). JavaPoet represents `?` with `upperBounds = [Object.class]`, which the code handles by generating `Types.subtypeOf(Object.class)`. A test case for this would confirm the generated code is correct and compilable.
v2:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds wildcard type handling (`? extends T` / `? super T`) to the `makeType` method in AutoValueMoshiExtension, generating appropriate `Types.subtypeOf()` / `Types.supertypeOf()` calls. The implementation is correct, but the test changes introduce a coverage regression.

POINTS:
- [auto-value-moshi/src/test/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtensionTest.java:75] **Test coverage regression**: The `simple()` test replaced `Set<String>` with `Set<? extends String>` and `Map<String, Set<String>>` with `Map<String, Set<? super String>>`. This means the previously tested non-wildcard cases — single-parameter generics (`Set<T>`) and nested non-wildcard parameterized types (`Map<String, Set<T>>`) — are NO LONGER COVERED by any test. Consider keeping the original non-wildcard properties alongside the new wildcard ones (e.g., rename `f`/`g` to `f2`/`g2` or add new properties) to preserve regression coverage for non-wildcard generics.
- [auto-value-moshi/src/main/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtension.java:501] **Error message during annotation processing**: The `IllegalArgumentException` thrown for unrepresentable wildcards (multiple bounds) will crash annotation processing. Consider emitting a proper compiler error via `Messager.printMessage(Diagnostic.Kind.ERROR, ...)` instead, so users get a clear error tied to the problematic property rather than a stack trace. Alternatively, add a test case for this scenario (e.g., `? extends T & Serializable`) to validate the error path is reachable and documented.
- [auto-value-moshi/src/main/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtension.java:495-505] The wildcard handling logic is correct: checking `lowerBounds` first ensures `? super T` uses `supertypeOf()` (since `? super T` has `lowerBounds=[T]` and `upperBounds=[Object.class]`). An unbounded `?` would have `upperBounds=[Object.class]`, producing `Types.subtypeOf(Object.class)`, which is semantically equivalent. No issue here.
v3:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds wildcard type handling to the `makeType()` code generation method, using Moshi's `Types.subtypeOf()` and `Types.supertypeOf()` APIs. The core implementation is clean and the test updates are appropriate, but there's a significant Moshi version compatibility concern.

POINTS:
- [auto-value-moshi/src/main/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtension.java:490-504] **CRITICAL: Moshi version compatibility** — The project depends on Moshi 1.4.0 (per `dependencies.gradle` line 11), but `Types.subtypeOf(Class)` and `Types.supertypeOf(Class)` were added in Moshi 1.9.0. These methods do NOT exist in Moshi 1.4.0, so the generated code will fail to compile against the declared dependency. Either the Moshi dependency needs to be upgraded to ≥1.9.0, or an alternative approach is needed for older versions.

- [auto-value-moshi/src/main/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtension.java:502-503] The `IllegalArgumentException` for multi-bound wildcards is reasonable since `Types.newParameterizedType()` can't represent intersection types, but consider using `RuntimeException` instead — this is code-generation-time validation (not an argument passed incorrectly), and `IllegalArgumentException` can be misleading in that context.

- [auto-value-moshi/src/main/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtension.java:500] For unbounded wildcards like `List<?>`, JavaPoet's `WildcardTypeName` stores `Object` as the sole upper bound, so this generates `Types.subtypeOf(Object.class)`. While semantically correct, this is verbose compared to `Types.wildcardOf()` (if available) or could be documented as expected behavior.

- [auto-value-moshi/src/test/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtensionTest.java:75,78] The test changes update existing property types (`Set<String>` → `Set<? extends String>`, `Map<String, Set<String>>` → `Map<String, Set<? super String>>`) which provides good coverage for both `subtypeOf` and `supertypeOf`. Consider adding a dedicated test method named something like `handlesWildcardTypes()` to make the new capability explicit rather than embedded within the general `simple()` test.
v4:
SUMMARY:
PR #78 adds support for wildcard types (`? extends X` and `? super X`) in generic type arguments for AutoValue properties by mapping JavaPoet's `WildcardTypeName` to Moshi's `Types.subtypeOf()` and `Types.supertypeOf()` APIs, which are confirmed available in Moshi 1.4.0.

POINTS:
- [auto-value-moshi/src/main/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtension.java:503-505] The `IllegalArgumentException` for "unrepresentable wildcard types" (multiple bounds) is unreachable — Java's type system prohibits wildcards with multiple bounds, so JavaPoet would never construct such a `WildcardTypeName`. Consider replacing with a comment or using the `Messager` API for a proper compile-time diagnostic.
- [auto-value-moshi/src/test/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtensionTest.java:75-76] The PR modifies existing tests but lacks a dedicated test case for bare unbounded wildcards (`Set<?> x()`). Since `WildcardTypeName.get()` yields `upperBounds = [Object]`, it would emit `Types.subtypeOf(Object.class)` — add explicit coverage for this path.
- [auto-value-moshi/src/test/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtensionTest.java:100-101] Constructor parameter formatting changed inconsistently: original had `e,      \nSet<String>` (extra spaces), new expectation has `e, \n     Set<? extends String>` (different indent). Normalize to match project formatting conventions.
- [auto-value-moshi/src/main/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtension.java:496] The cast `(WildcardTypeName) typeArg` is safe per the `instanceof` guard on line 494, but the pattern could be cleaner with Java 16+ `instanceof` pattern matching if the project targets that baseline.
- [auto-value-moshi/src/main/java/com/ryanharter/auto/value/moshi/AutoValueMoshiExtension.java:490-507] Correct use of Moshi's `Types.supertypeOf()` and `Types.subtypeOf()` — confirmed available in Moshi 1.4.0. The logic properly prioritizes lower bounds (`? super X`) before upper bounds (`? extends X`), which is the right semantic ordering.

Overall: Implementation is correct and resolves #77. Main suggestions: add a bare `?` wildcard test case and reconsider the defensive error handling strategy.
